title: Rebutia melanocentra
intro: Rebutia melanocentra is a species of flowering cactus, native to Bolivia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and dark green stems. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Rebutia melanocentra include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rebutia melanocentra include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Rebutia melanocentra belongs to the family Cactaceae and is a species of cactus. It is further classified into the subfamily Cactoideae and tribe Rebutieae. This classification places Rebutia melanocentra within the larger group of cacti. The Rebutieae tribe is a relatively small group of cacti that includes Rebutia and Sulcorebutia species. The classification of Rebutia melanocentra is subject to change based on ongoing botanical research and discoveries.
